personality_one_liner: A high-density, authoritative intelligence dashboard built like an operational command center.

spacing.rule: Use rigid grids, hard alignment, visible spacing systems instead of soft cards. Intentional whitespace imbalance.

dont's: No blur or glassmorphism. This is a brutalist app. Use stark contrasts and borders.

screens.name: Live Viral Claims Feed
screens.purpose: A real-time ticker and list of incoming social posts needing fact-checking, heavily stylized like a command center.

states.loading: Skeleton loaders matching the rigid grid layout, monochromatic.
states.empty: A mono-spaced 'NO DATA STREAM' alert over a stark background with a manual refresh CTA.
states.error: Hard red error box with a bold 'CONNECTION FAILED' message and a sharp retry button.

screens.layout: Streamlined vertical list layout with high info density. Top of screen contains a heavy, bordered Stats Overview block (Total, Verified, Debunked). Each post is a sharp-edged card with a 2pt borderStrong outline. Display platform badges (X, Instagram, TikTok), content snippet, mono timestamp, and a status chip. Use pull-to-refresh for real-time updates.

screens.name: Verified/Debunked Dashboard
screens.purpose: A grid-based archive displaying fact-checked posts with definitive visual verdicts and reel previews.

states.empty: An empty state showing an abstract newsroom illustration with 'NO VERIFIED CLAIMS' text.

screens.layout: Stacked grid view. Cards showing fact-checked posts with a thick progress bar or gauge for the confidence score (Red for <0.5, Amber for 0.5-0.95, Green for >=0.95). Verdict banner, logic breakdown text, source citations. A large section dedicated to the generated 9:16 reel preview (using the newsroom placeholder image).

screens.name: Admin Queue
screens.purpose: Moderation list for borderline posts (Score < 0.95) needing human review, focused on operational speed.

states.empty: Large mono text 'QUEUE CLEAR. NO HUMAN REVIEW REQUIRED.'

screens.layout: Minimal card layout with content cards. Each card displays the confidence score prominently, raw payload inspector shown in a mono-font block (SpaceMono), and hard-edged Approve (Green) / Reject (Red) buttons at the bottom. The layout must feel mechanical, utilizing thick structural borders and exposed system states.

screens.name: Post Detail View
screens.purpose: Deep dive into a specific post's fact-check logic and generation status.

states.loading: Skeleton UI blocks for each section.

screens.layout: Full screen modal or stack screen. Oversized typography for the claim text. Split panels: AI logic breakdown on one side, confidence score (huge font) on the other. Render job status (queued, rendering, completed) displayed as a step-by-step progress tracker.

reel_preview_placeholder.alt: Empty modern newsroom studio background for Reel previews

instructions_to_main_agent: CRITICAL: The design language is strictly Brutalist Mobile LIGHT. No rounded corners (radius 0 everywhere), no drop shadows, no glassmorphism, no blurs.
Borders are your primary layout tool. Use 'borderStrong' (#111111) for 2pt sharp outlines on cards and structural sections.
Use 'SpaceMono' for all timestamps, raw payload blocks, and stats indicators to enforce the intelligence command-center vibe.

Confidence Scores are critical to the UX: <0.5 is Red (Error), 0.5-0.95 is Amber (Warning), >=0.95 is Green (Success). Do NOT use any color other than these semantic colors and the monochrome palette.

Social badges (X, Insta, TikTok) can use Expo Vector Icons (Ionicons/FontAwesome) as primary since the image search returned phone mockups, but use the provided image URLs as fallback graphics where appropriate. Explicitly style X as black, Insta as pink-gradient (if possible via Expo LinearGradient or just solid pink), TikTok as cyan/black.
Ensure 'Live Viral Claims Feed' has a top sticky section for total stats.
The 'raw payload inspector' in the Admin Queue must look like a raw JSON code block with mono typography.
